name: Japanese, Bachelor of Arts

requirements.heading: Preparatory Subject Matter
notes: Equivalent as determined by a language placement exam.

requirements.heading: Depth Subject Matter
notes: Grading: Students may take up to two and no more than two (regular, letter-graded) courses for the major on a P/NP basis. Courses which are only offered on a P/NP basis do not count toward these limits.

Note: With prior approval of the undergraduate advisor, students already proficient in Japanese at any third-year level1 must take other upper division Japanese (JPN) courses to replace language course(s).
Up to two (8 units) upper division Chinese courses
Other advanced literature and culture courses selected in consultation with the undergraduate advisor.

advising_notes: Honors Program: To be eligible to receive high or highest honors in the Japanese major, students must complete a senior thesis project. A student interested in pursuing a senior thesis project must enroll in JPN 194H and complete a scholarly paper or similar research project under the direction of a senate faculty member.
